rag_rules: Insert generated AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports are placed in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
